description: Validate brittle or destructible rigid-body fracture as a contact-energy threshold invariant rather than a glass-specific visual template.
physical_assumptions: A brittle body has a declared fracture threshold and minimum fragment evidence requirement.
Fracture may occur only after runtime contact or impact evidence with sufficient impact energy.
Below-threshold impacts should not produce fracture events unless an alternate force source is explicitly labeled.
Fragments are runtime evidence and must be represented in artifacts, not only rendered as visual debris.
required_assets: fracture fragment mesh or analytic fragment proxy
verifier_rules: Brittle object declares a positive fracture_threshold_j.
Expected impactor-brittle contact pair appears in contact events.
Impact energy is present and exceeds threshold when fracture occurs.
Fracture frame is not earlier than the causal contact frame.
Fragment count is at least expected_min_fragment_count.
repair_suggestions: Add fracture_threshold_j and expected_min_fragment_count to expected_physics.
Export contact impact energy from the runtime before checking fracture behavior.
Move any pre-contact break animation into a post-contact fracture event.
Use analytic fragment proxy for smoke if a real destructible mesh is unavailable, and mark it as proxy.
